devanagari: गंगावतरणकथा भगीरथवृत्तं सौदासचरितं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । अंशुमांश्च तपस्तेपे गंगानयनकाम्यया । कालं महान्तं नाशक्नोत् ततः कालेन संस्थितः ॥ १ ...

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(Like his grandfather Sagara) Aṃśumān (also renounced his kingdom in favour of his son Dilīpa), and practised penance for a long time, with the desire of bringing the Gaṅgā down on the earth. But he was not successful (in his mission). In course of time, he passed away.

devanagari: दिलीपस्तत्सुतस्तद्वद् अशक्तः कालमेयिवान् । भगीरथस्तस्य पुत्रः तेपे स सुमहत् तपः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: dilīpastatsutastadvad aśaktaḥ kālameyivān bhagīrathastasya putraḥ tepe sa sumahat tapaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: His son Dilīpa also proved unsuccessful, and met death (in his attempt to bring down Gaṅgā). His son Bhāgīratha performed very severe austerities.

devanagari: दर्शयामास तं देवी प्रसन्ना वरदास्मि ते । इत्युक्तः स्वं अभिप्रायं शशंसावनतो नृपः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: darśayāmāsa taṃ devī prasannā varadāsmi te ityuktaḥ svaṃ abhiprāyaṃ śaśaṃsāvanato nṛpaḥ

translation: The goddess (Gaṅgā) manifested herself to him and said, “I am pleased (with your penance). I shall bestow on you the boon (desired by you).” Being assured thus, the king bowed low (in humility), and submitted to her his object (of performing the penance).

devanagari: कोऽपि धारयिता वेगं पतन्त्या मे महीतले । अन्यथा भूतलं भित्त्वा नृप यास्ये रसातलम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ko anyathā bhūtalaṃ bhittvā nṛpa yāsye rasātalam

translation: (She replied:) “Is there anyone capable of sustaining my force while I descend on the earth. Otherwise, I shall penetrate the crust of the earth and go down to Rasātala, the 6th sub-terranean region, Oh King.

devanagari: किं चाहं न भुवं यास्ये नरा मय्यामृजन्त्यघम् । मृजामि तदघं क्वाहं राजन् तत्र विचिन्त्यताम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ cāhaṃ na bhuvaṃ yāsye narā mayyāmṛjantyagham mṛjāmi tadaghaṃ kvāhaṃ rājan tatra vicintyatām

translation: Besides, I am not willing to go to the earth. Men will wash of their sins in me. Where (and how) can I absolve myself of that sin? Some remedy on this should also be considered, Oh King.”

devanagari: श्रीभगीरथ उवाच । साधवो न्यासिनः शान्ता ब्रह्मिष्ठा लोकपावनाः । हरन्ति अघं ते अंगसंगात् तेष्वास्ते ह्यघभित् हरिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagīratha uvāca sādhavo nyāsinaḥ śāntā brahmiṣṭhā lokapāvanāḥ haranti aghaṃ te aṃgasaṃgāt teṣvāste hyaghabhit hariḥ

[nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation:
Bhagīratha said:
[1] “Righteous persons who have renounced actions (for obtaining worldly objects), and have controlled their senseorgans, and persons devoted to the contemplation of Brahman, (or prominent knowers of Brahman), are themselves capable of purifying the world. By the contact of their body (while bathing in you) they will annihilate your sins, as Lord Hari, the destroyer of sins, abides in them.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī: Pious persons endowed with quiescence, self-control etc., completely free from desires (for heaven, pleasures etc.), thoroughly devoted to meditation of Brahman and thus established in Brahman and purifiers of the world, will annihilate your sins by their physical contact (e.g. washing hands or feet in your waters or by ablutions).
Bhāgavata Candrikā and Bālaprabodhini: As Lord Hari, the destroyer of sins, abides in them, the question of transfer of your sins by them to others does not arise, as Hari annihilates the sins altogether.

devanagari: धारयिष्यति ते वेगं रुद्रस्त्वात्मा शरीरिणाम् । यस्मिन् ओतं इदं प्रोतं विश्वं शाटीव तन्तुषु ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dhārayiṣyati te vegaṃ rudrastvātmā śarīriṇām yasmin otaṃ idaṃ protaṃ viśvaṃ śāṭīva tantuṣu
translation:
God Rudra, who is the very Soul of corporeal beings, and in Whom is woven this universe, warp and woof, as threads are interwoven in a piece of cloth, will bear your force.”
7(A). Having heard this speech of Bhagīratha, Gaṅgā advised, “In that case, propitiate the great god Śiva, for sustaining the velocity of my waters.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा स नृपो देवं तपसा तोषयच्छिवम् । कालेनाल्पीयसा राजन् तस्येशः समतुष्यत ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā sa nṛpo devaṃ tapasā toṣayacchivam kālenālpīyasā rājan tasyeśaḥ samatuṣyata

translation: Having spoken thus,[2] the king propitiated god Śiva by his penance. Within a short period (of penance), Lord Śiva was also highly pleased with him.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
v.l.: tac chrutvā—Hearing this—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: तथेति राज्ञाभिहितं सर्वलोकहितः शिवः । दधारावहितो गंगां पादपूतजलां हरेः ॥ ९ ॥

translation: Granting the prayer of the King with the words, “Be it so”, Lord Śiva who is always benevolent to the world, carefully bore on his head the Gaṅgā whose waters are hallowed by the feet of Hari.

devanagari: भगीरथः स राजर्षिः निन्ये भुवनपावनीम् । यत्र स्वपितॄणां देहा भस्मीभूताः स्म शेरते ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: bhagīrathaḥ sa rājarṣiḥ ninye bhuvanapāvanīm yatra svapitṝṇāṃ dehā bhasmībhūtāḥ sma śerate

translation: The royal sage Bhagīratha led Gaṅgā, the purifier of the Universe, to the spot where the bodies of his fore-fathers (great-grand-uncles), lay reduced to ashes.

devanagari: रथेन वायुवेगेन प्रयान्तं अनुधावती । देशान् पुनन्ती निर्दग्धान् आसिञ्चत् सगरात्मजान् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: rathena vāyuvegena prayāntaṃ anudhāvatī deśān punantī nirdagdhān āsiñcat sagarātmajān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ pū class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ pū class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ pū class 9 verb]

translation: (Closely on the heels of) Bhagīratha who rode (in advance) in a chariot speeding with the velocity of the wind she rushed after him, hallowing the countries or regions (through which she flowed), and bathed the burnt-up sons of Sagara (with her waters).

devanagari: यज्जलस्पर्शमात्रेण ब्रह्मदण्डहता अपि । सगरात्मजा दिवं जग्मुः केवलं देहभस्मभिः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yajjalasparśamātreṇa brahmadaṇḍahatā api sagarātmajā divaṃ jagmuḥ kevalaṃ dehabhasmabhiḥ

translation: The sons of Sagara, though struck down by their offence against a Brāhmaṇa (sage Kapila). attained to the celestial regions simply through the contact of her waters with their ashes.

devanagari: भस्मीभूतांगसंगेन स्वर्याताः सगरात्मजाः । किं पुनः श्रद्धया देवीं सेवन्ते ये धृतव्रताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhasmībhūtāṃgasaṃgena svaryātāḥ sagarātmajāḥ kiṃ punaḥ śraddhayā devīṃ sevante ye dhṛtavratāḥ
translation: Merely by the contact of her waters with the ashes to which their bodies were reduced, the sons of Sagara ascended the heaven. What need be said in case of those who observe religious vows and serve her with faith?

devanagari: न ह्येतत् परमाश्चर्यं स्वर्धुन्या यदिहोदितम् । अनन्तचरणाम्भोज प्रसूताया भवच्छिदः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyetat paramāścaryaṃ svardhunyā yadihoditam anantacaraṇāmbhoja prasūtāyā bhavacchidaḥ

translation: What has been glorified above about (the hallowing power of) the heavenly river is not very miraculous, inasmuch as She springs from the lotus-like feet of the Infinite Lord (Viṣṇu) and puts an end to Saṃsāra.

devanagari: सन्निवेश्य मनो यस्मिन् श्रद्धया मुनयोऽमलाः । त्रैगुण्यं दुस्त्यजं हित्वा सद्यो यातास्तदात्मताम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sanniveśya mano yasmin śraddhayā munayo traiguṇyaṃ dustyajaṃ hitvā sadyo yātāstadātmatām

translation: Concentrating their minds properly with faith in Him, pure-minded sages shed off their contacts with (and attachment to) the three guṇas of Prakṛti (and its product—their body,—misidentified with the soul)—which is so very difficult to cast off—and have instantly attained identity with Brahman.

devanagari: श्रुतो भगीरथाज्जज्ञे तस्य नाभोऽपरोऽभवत् । सिन्धुद्वीपः ततस्तस्मात् अयुतायुः ततोऽभवत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śruto bhagīrathājjajñe tasya nābho sindhudvīpaḥ tatastasmāt ayutāyuḥ tato
translation: Śruta was born from Bhagīratha who had another son named Nābha. His son was Sindhudvīpa from whom was born Ayutāyu.

devanagari: ऋतूपर्णो नलसखो योऽश्वविद्यामयान्नलात् । दत्त्वाक्षहृदयं चास्मै सर्वकामस्तु तत्सुतम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛtūparṇo nalasakho yo dattvākṣahṛdayaṃ cāsmai sarvakāmastu tatsutam

translation: Ayutāyu’s son was Ṛtuparṇa, a friend of Nala who, in exchange of imparting the secret technique of the play of dice to Nala, learnt from him the equestrian science (the secret of managing horses). His (Ṛtuparṇa’s) son was Sarvakāma.

devanagari: ततः सुदासः तत्पुत्रो मदयन्तीपतिर्नृपः । आहुर्मित्रसहं यं वै कल्माषाङ्घ्रिमुत क्वचित् । वसिष्ठशापाद्रक्षोऽभूत् अनपत्यः स्वकर्मणा ॥ १८ ॥

translation: From his loins was born Sudās whose son was the husband of Madayantī, Oh King. People called him Mitrasaha, and rarely as Kalmāṣapāda. He became a demon through the curse of Vasiṣṭha, but remained issueless inconsequence of his own act.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । किं निमित्तो गुरोः शापः सौदासस्य महात्मनः । एतद् वेदितुमिच्छामः कथ्यतां न रहो यदि ॥ १९ ॥

translation:
King (Parīkṣit) asked:
We wish to know the reason or the occasion of the pronouncement of the curse by preceptor Vasiṣṭha on the great- souled son of Sudās. Be pleased to narrate it to us, if there be no secret about it.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । सौदासो मृगयां किञ्चित् चरन् रक्षो जघान ह । मुमोच भ्रातरं सोऽथ गतः प्रतिचिकीर्षया ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca saudāso mṛgayāṃ kiñcit caran rakṣo jaghāna ha mumoca bhrātaraṃ so

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
As the tradition goes, king Saudāsa (the son of Sudās) happened to kill a certain demon, while on hunting expedition. He allowed his brother to escape. He (the Rākṣasa’s brother) went away with a determination to wreak revenge.

devanagari: सञ्चिन्तयन् अघं राज्ञः सूदरूपधरो गृहे । गुरवे भोक्तुकामाय पक्त्वा निन्ये नरामिषम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sañcintayan aghaṃ rājñaḥ sūdarūpadharo gṛhe gurave bhoktukāmāya paktvā ninye narāmiṣam

translation: Remembering the offence (of killing his brother) committed by the king, and disguising himself as the cook of the king in the palace, he dressed human flesh and presented it for service to the king’s preceptor Vasiṣṭha, who came there to dine.

devanagari: परिवेक्ष्यमाणं भगवान् विलोक्य अभक्ष्यमञ्जसा । राजानं अशपत् क्रुद्धो रक्षो ह्येवं भविष्यसि ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: parivekṣyamāṇaṃ bhagavān vilokya abhakṣyamañjasā rājānaṃ aśapat kruddho rakṣo hyevaṃ bhaviṣyasi

translation: Perceiving easily that a prohibited article of food (human flesh) was about to be served to him, the venerable sage flared up in rage, and cursed the king, “You will turn into an ogre by this improper act of yours.”

devanagari: रक्षःकृतं तद्विदित्वा चक्रे द्वादशवार्षिकम् । सोऽपि अपोऽञ्जलिनादाय गुरुं शप्तुं समुद्यतः ॥ २३ ॥

translation: Having come to know that, that act (serving of human flesh) was committed by a demon (and that the king was innocent), he limited the duration of his curse to (a period of twelve years. But (in the meanwhile) Saudāsa took water into the hollow of his joined palms and was ready to counter-curse his preceptor (who uttered the unjustifiable imprecation).

english_devnagari: vārito madayantyāpo ruśatīḥ pādayorjahau diśaḥ khamavanīṃ sarvaṃ paśyan jīvamayaṃ nṛpaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He was however prevented (from cursing his preceptor) by (his queen) Madayantī. The king found that all the cardinal points, the sky, the earth were all instinct with life. (And lest he should injure living beings), he let the water be charged with wrathful mantras, fall on his own feet (and thus justified his epithet—mitra-saha, indulgent towards friends).

devanagari: राक्षसं भावमापन्नः पादे कल्माषतां गतः । व्यवायकाले ददृशे वनौकोदम्पती द्विजौ ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: rākṣasaṃ bhāvamāpannaḥ pāde kalmāṣatāṃ gataḥ vyavāyakāle dadṛśe vanaukodampatī dvijau
translation: Changed into a demonic state, and with both of his feet turned black (by the water charged by him with a curse), he happened to see a forest-dwelling couple of Brāhmaṇas in the act of sexual intercourse.

devanagari: क्षुधार्तो जगृहे विप्रं तत्पत्‍न्याहाकृतार्थवत् । न भवान् राक्षसः साक्षात् इक्ष्वाकूणां महारथः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣudhārto jagṛhe vipraṃ tatpat‍nyāhākṛtārthavat na bhavān rākṣasaḥ sākṣāt ikṣvākūṇāṃ mahārathaḥ
translation: Overpowered with hunger, he caught hold of the Brāhmaṇa, when his wife helplessly appealed to him, “Your honour is not a born ogre. You are veritably a great hero (a chariot-warrior) belonging directly to the race of Ikṣvākus.

devanagari: मदयन्त्याः पतिर्वीर नाधर्मं कर्तुमर्हसि । देहि मेऽपत्यकामाया अकृतार्थं पतिं द्विजम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: madayantyāḥ patirvīra nādharmaṃ kartumarhasi dehi me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ mad ], [genitive single from √ mad ]

translation: Oh warrior! You are the husband of Madayantī. It does not behove you to perpetrate unrighteous deeds. Be pleased to restore to me who am desirous of having a child, the Brāhmaṇa, my husband, who has not accomplished his purpose.

devanagari: देहोऽयं मानुषो राजन् पुरुषस्याखिलार्थदः । तस्मादस्य वधो वीर सर्वार्थवध उच्यते ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: deho tasmādasya vadho vīra sarvārthavadha ucyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh King! This human body is capable of securing all the objects (puruṣārthas) in human life. Hence the destruction of this body is called the total destruction of all purposes.

devanagari: एष हि ब्राह्मणो विद्वान् तपःशीलगुणान्वितः । आरिराधयिषुर्ब्रह्म महापुरुषसंज्ञितम् । सर्वभूतात्मभावेन भूतेष्वन्तर्हितं गुणैः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa hi brāhmaṇo vidvān tapaḥśīlaguṇānvitaḥ ārirādhayiṣurbrahma mahāpuruṣasaṃjñitam sarvabhūtātmabhāvena bhūteṣvantarhitaṃ guṇaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: This Brāhmaṇa is learned and endowed with asceticism, virtuous character and other qualities. He desires to propitiate Brahman which is designated as Mahāpuruṣa (Viṣṇu) who abides in all beings as their Inner Controller, but lies obscured in them on account of guṇas.

devanagari: सोऽयं ब्रह्मर्षिवर्यस्ते राजर्षिप्रवराद् विभो । कथमर्हति धर्मज्ञ वधं पितुरिवात्मजः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: so kathamarhati dharmajña vadhaṃ piturivātmajaḥ
translation: This being the case, how does this prominent Brāhmaṇa sage deserve death at the hands of a great royal sage like you, even as the death of a son at the hands of the father, Oh knower of the righteous path?

devanagari: तस्य साधोरपापस्य भ्रूणस्य ब्रह्मवादिनः । कथं वधं यथा बभ्रोः मन्यते सन्मतो भवान् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya sādhorapāpasya bhrūṇasya brahmavādinaḥ kathaṃ vadhaṃ yathā babhroḥ manyate sanmato bhavān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Your worship is respected by the righteous. How do you approve of the death of the righteous, sinless (innocent) Śrotriya who is well-versed in the Vedas (or innocent like a child in the womb), any more than killing of a cow.

devanagari: यइ अयं क्रियते भक्षः तर्हि मां खाद पूर्वतः । न जीविष्ये विना येन क्षणं च मृतकं यथा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yai ayaṃ kriyate bhakṣaḥ tarhi māṃ khāda pūrvataḥ na jīviṣye vinā yena kṣaṇaṃ ca mṛtakaṃ yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: If he is to be your victim, please devour me first, inasmuch as I shall not live without him for a minute, and am as good as dead.”

devanagari: एवं करुणभाषिण्या विलपन्त्या अनाथवत् । व्याघ्रः पशुमिवाखादत् सौदासः शापमोहितः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ karuṇabhāṣiṇyā vilapantyā anāthavat vyāghraḥ paśumivākhādat saudāsaḥ śāpamohitaḥ
translation: Disregarding the piteous appeal of the helpless bewailing Brāhmaṇa woman, Saudāsa deluded by (his preceptor’s) curse, devoured the Brāhmaṇa, as a tiger would prey upon a beast.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणी वीक्ष्य दिधिषुं पुरुषादेन भक्षितम् । शोचन्ति आत्मानमुर्वीशं अशपत् कुपिता सती ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇī vīkṣya didhiṣuṃ puruṣādena bhakṣitam śocanti ātmānamurvīśaṃ aśapat kupitā satī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhakṣ ], [accusative single from √ bhakṣ ]

translation: Finding her impregnator (husband) eaten up by a cannibal, the Brāhmaṇa woman lamented herself. Flying in a rage, the chaste lady uttered the imprecation on the king.

devanagari: यस्मान्मे भक्षितः पाप कामार्तायाः पतिस्त्वया । तवापि मृत्युः आधानाद् अकृतप्रज्ञ दर्शितः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmānme bhakṣitaḥ pāpa kāmārtāyāḥ patistvayā tavāpi mṛtyuḥ ādhānād akṛtaprajña darśitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]

translation: “You wicked fellow! Inasmuch as my husband has been devoured by you while I was afflicted with sexual desire, your death too is indicated to follow while -engaged in sexual intercourse, you foolish one.”

devanagari: एवं मित्रसहं शप्त्वा पतिलोकपरायणा । तदस्थीनि समिद्धेऽग्नौ प्रास्य भर्तुर्गतिं गता ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ mitrasahaṃ śaptvā patilokaparāyaṇā tadasthīni samiddhe
translation: Having thus cursed king Mitrasaha, the woman who was determined to attain to the region of her husband, consigned to flames his bones in a burning fire, and followed the course of her husband (by immolating herself to fire).

devanagari: विशापो द्वादशाब्दान्ते मैथुनाय समुद्यतः । विज्ञाय ब्राह्मणीशापं महिष्या स निवारितः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: viśāpo dvādaśābdānte maithunāya samudyataḥ vijñāya brāhmaṇīśāpaṃ mahiṣyā sa nivāritaḥ
translation: When freed from the curse at the end of the twelfth year, he (Saudāsa) proceeded to have sexual intercourse (with his queen), but the queen who knew well the imprecation of the Brāhmaṇa lady prevented him from that attempt.

devanagari: अत ऊर्ध्वं स तत्याज स्त्रीसुखं कर्मणाप्रजाः । वसिष्ठस्तदनुज्ञातो मदयन्त्यां प्रजामधात् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: ata ūrdhvaṃ sa tatyāja strīsukhaṃ karmaṇāprajāḥ vasiṣṭhastadanujñāto madayantyāṃ prajāmadhāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Thenceforth, he gave up all pleasures with women and he remained issueless by this act. It was with his consent that the sage Vasiṣṭha begot an issue on queen Madayantī.

devanagari: सा वै सप्त समा गर्भं अबिभ्रन्न व्यजायत । जघ्नेऽश्मनोदरं तस्याः सोऽश्मकस्तेन कथ्यते ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sā vai sapta samā garbhaṃ abibhranna vyajāyata jaghne

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: For seven years she carried the foetus and did not deliver. Vasiṣṭha then hit her abdomen with a stone, and the son so born came to be known as Aśmaka.

devanagari: अश्मकान् मूलको जज्ञे यः स्त्रीभिः परिरक्षितः । नारीकवच इत्युक्तो निःक्षत्रे मूलकोऽभवत् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: aśmakān mūlako jajñe yaḥ strībhiḥ parirakṣitaḥ nārīkavaca ityukto niḥkṣatre mūlako

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: From Aśmaka was born Mūlaka who was protected on all sides (from Paraśurāma) by women, and hence he is called Nārīkavaca (women-armoured). He is called Mūlaka as he was the root of further pro-creation of the Kṣatriya race, when it was being exterminated by Paraśurāma.

devanagari: ततो दशरथस्तस्मात् पुत्र ऐडविडिस्ततः । राजा विश्वसहो यस्य खट्वांगश्चक्रवर्त्यभूत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato daśarathastasmāt putra aiḍaviḍistataḥ rājā viśvasaho yasya khaṭvāṃgaścakravartyabhūt
translation: From Mūlaka sprang forth Daśaratha of whom a son, Aiḍaviḍa was born. His son was King Viśvasaha whose son Khaṭvāṅga became the sovereign ruler of the world.

devanagari: यो देवैरर्थितो दैत्यान् अवधीद् युधि दुर्जयः । मुहूर्तं आयुः ज्ञात्वैत्य स्वपुरं सन्दधे मनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yo devairarthito daityān avadhīd yudhi durjayaḥ muhūrtaṃ āyuḥ jñātvaitya svapuraṃ sandadhe manaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Being entreated by gods, the invincible king killed a number of demons in the battle. Learning (from gods) that only one muhūrta (48 minutes) of his life is left, he hurried back to his capital (in a celestial car) and concentrated his mind on the Supreme Lord.

devanagari: न मे ब्रह्मकुलात् प्राणाः कुलदैवान्न चात्मजाः । न श्रियो न मही राज्यं न दाराश्चातिवल्लभाः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: na me brahmakulāt prāṇāḥ kuladaivānna cātmajāḥ na śriyo na mahī rājyaṃ na dārāścātivallabhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]

translation: (He mused to himself). Neither my life nor my sons, nor my wealth nor the earth, nor my empire or my queens are dearer to me than the Brāhmaṇa race who form our family-deities.

devanagari: न बाल्येऽपि मतिर्मह्यं अधर्मे रमते क्वचित् । नापश्यं उत्तमश्लोकात् अन्यत् किञ्चन वस्त्वहम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: na bālye nāpaśyaṃ uttamaślokāt anyat kiñcana vastvaham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: Not even in my childhood, my mind ever took delight in unrighteousness. I did not look upon anything other than the Lord of hallowed reputation (Viṣṇu) as the reality.

devanagari: देवैः कामवरो दत्तो मह्यं त्रिभुवनेश्वरैः । न वृणे तमहं कामं भूतभावनभावनः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: devaiḥ kāmavaro datto mahyaṃ tribhuvaneśvaraiḥ na vṛṇe tamahaṃ kāmaṃ bhūtabhāvanabhāvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: A boon of my choice was offered for bestowal on me by gods, the rulers of the three worlds. But as I contemplate on the creator of all beings, I do not seek any object of desire.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
As the boon will come in the way of my realization of the Lord—VR. Gd. Bhaktamanorañjanī
Any act except the propitiation of Hari, is not acceptable to me as it is not conducive to Liberation—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: ये विक्षिप्तेन्द्रियधियो देवास्ते स्वहृदि स्थितम् । न विन्दन्ति प्रियं शश्वद् आत्मानं किमुतापरे ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ye vikṣiptendriyadhiyo devāste svahṛdi sthitam na vindanti priyaṃ śaśvad ātmānaṃ kimutāpare

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: The celestials themselves have got their minds and senses distracted (by objects of senses), and do not realize the beloved, eternal Soul who stays established in their own heart. Need it be said that human and other beings, characterised by rajas and tamas, can never do so.

devanagari: अथेशमायारचितेषु संगं । गुणेषु गन्धर्वपुरोपमेषु । रूढं प्रकृत्यात्मनि विश्वकर्तुः । भावेन हित्वा तमहं प्रपद्ये ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: atheśamāyāraciteṣu saṃgaṃ guṇeṣu gandharvapuropameṣu rūḍhaṃ prakṛtyātmani viśvakartuḥ bhāvena hitvā tamahaṃ prapadye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ruh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ruh class 1 verb]

translation: I shall give up, through deep devotion to the Creator of the Universe, attachment to guṇas (the product of guṇas viz. the object of senses) which has been firmly fixed in the mind, by the very nature of things, but which are the creation of the Illusive Power (Māyā) of the Lord, and hence are as unreal and transitory as the city of Gandharvas in the sky, and take asylum in him.

devanagari: इति व्यवसितो बुद्ध्या नारायणगृहीतया । हित्वान्यभावमज्ञानं ततः स्वं भावमास्थितः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasito buddhyā nārāyaṇagṛhītayā hitvānyabhāvamajñānaṃ tataḥ svaṃ bhāvamāsthitaḥ
translation: Having determined thus by his reasoning capacity and intellect, which was attracted towards himself (and thus possessed) by Lord Nārāyaṇa, he discarded ignorance[4] and the consequent false notions of the identity of the body with Soul and attained to his essential nature (which is Brahmahood).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
hitvānyabhavam ajñānam—Having given up his body.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: यत्तद्‍ब्रह्म परं सूक्ष्मं अशून्यं शून्यकल्पितम् । भगवान् वासुदेवेति यं गृणन्ति हि सात्वताः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: yattad‍brahma paraṃ sūkṣmaṃ aśūnyaṃ śūnyakalpitam bhagavān vāsudeveti yaṃ gṛṇanti hi sātvatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kḷp ], [accusative single from √ kḷp ]

translation:
[5] (The state) that is the Supreme Brahman which is very subtle but not a void[6] though it is as good as intangible, as it is beyond the rage of expression, and which the devotees of Lord Viṣṇu sing as the glorious Lord Vāsudeva.
[back to top]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī: The stage to which Veḍantins call the Supreme Brahman, the yogins describe as very subtle, beyond the ken of senses like the sky. Some non-vedic thinkers (viz. the Buddhists, especially the followers of Mādhya- mika school) assume it as void; but it is really positive (and not void) and which the followers of the Pañcarātra school (Sātvatas) call Lord Vasudeva.
Kramasandarbha. With his intellect (mind) possessed by Nārāyaṇa, Khaṭvāṅga attained to Brahmā’s region in a muhūrta.

Footnote [6]:
Bhaktamanorañjanī aśūnya—(i) Proved as positively existent and not non-existent like horns of a hare.
(ii) Śūnya is destitute of guṇas, attributeless, a-śūnya means full of infinite excellences, conducive to the good of all or devoid of guṇas which deserve to be discarded.
Sva-bhāva: The brahmabhāva implies devoted service to the Lord as of a chaste woman to her husband.
